title: Allow migrating graphroot
body:
### Feature request description

An user might want to migrate `podman`'s graphroot (e.g. the user is forced to rename a directory #24938, or the user want to move the storage dir to a new volume). As of now that is impossible, moving the directory and changing the `graphroot` in `storage.conf` will not work because the database still contains static path pointing to the old graphroot.

Right now, if an user wants to change graphroot, they have to either reset and recreate the containers, or manually exploring and editing the sqlite databases used by podman, which is error prone.

### Suggest potential solution

Podman could had a subcommand to migrate the location of the graphroot, while keeping databases and configurations consistent. Alternatively, a subcommand to check the consistency of static paths, and fix up the broken paths would be fine.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

An alternative would be bind mounting the new location on `/var/lib/containers/storage`. But that is just a work around and might be a source of confusion. It also will not play well for rootless users, where bind mounting the new location onto the old one might mean mounting the user's home into another user's home.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: If we wanted to do this, the only way I can think of is to take the paths we're storing in the database and replace whatever the graphroot is with `$GRAPHROOT` (and similar for our other paths - runroot, tmpdir, static dir, volume path) before we store, and convert back to a real path when we retrieve. This'd be a pretty major change to how we do things and would only work for new containers (thus, new installations).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
